rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of uncertainty about whether the differentials are still holding between interventions
rows.imp_rat: Recurring uncertainty about whether differentials still hold between interventions is a persistent emotional cost to the guardian role.
rows.sat_rat: Continuous trending and alarms give fairly good ongoing confidence between visits in the status quo.

rows.stmt: Reduce the likelihood of being perceived as having overspent or wasted capital on contamination-control infrastructure
rows.imp_rat: Being seen as having overspent on contamination-control capital pressures the director's steward identity from the cost-discipline side.
rows.sat_rat: Cost-justification norms give reasonable cover against overspend perceptions, so this is somewhat better handled than the underfunding risks.

rows.stmt: Reduce the amount of conditioned air over-supplied to sustain control margin beyond what the cascade actually requires
rows.imp_rat: Over-supplying conditioned air to hold margin matters to energy targets he is increasingly asked to honor, but it is subordinate to guaranteeing the pressure hierarchy holds.
rows.sat_rat: Conservative over-supply is the default status-quo safety habit, so current practice sacrifices energy efficiency to protect margin rather than optimizing it.

rows.stk: Cleanroom HVAC / Building Management System Operator
rows.source_job: feel confident the alarm is real
rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of doubt about whether a sounding pressure alarm reflects a genuine breach or a false signal
rows.imp_rat: Doubt about whether an alarm is genuine adds cognitive load, moderately affecting the operator's confidence during a shift.
rows.sat_rat: Current alarms rarely carry confidence context, so doubt persists and must be resolved manually each time.

rows.stk: Cleanroom HVAC / Building Management System Operator
rows.source_job: feel confident the alarm is real
rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of overreacting to false pressure alarms that erodes the operator's trust in the alarm system
rows.imp_rat: Repeated overreaction to false alarms erodes trust in the system, a real morale and alarm-fatigue issue for the operator.
rows.sat_rat: Chronic nuisance alarms in many installations steadily erode operator trust, a well-known unresolved problem.

rows.stk: Cleanroom HVAC / Building Management System Operator
rows.source_job: feel in control of zone pressures
rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of unpredictability in how zones respond that leaves the operator feeling out of control
rows.imp_rat: Unpredictable zone responses that leave the operator feeling out of control undermine confident operation, though the emotional stakes trail the functional ones.
rows.sat_rat: Familiar systems give experienced operators a sense of control, but coupled-zone surprises still occur and erode that feeling.

rows.stk: Cleanroom HVAC / Building Management System Operator
rows.source_job: feel in control of zone pressures
rows.stmt: Reduce the time the operator is unsure whether every zone is currently holding its differential-pressure target
rows.imp_rat: Uncertainty about whether every zone is currently holding target is a background emotional stressor rather than a direct breach risk.
rows.sat_rat: Overview dashboards give at-a-glance status for many zones, though confidence dips when displays are cluttered or lagging.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of servicing dampers or seals that still have usable life remaining during preventive interventions
rows.imp_rat: Servicing components with usable life left is wasteful but low-risk to the cascade, so it matters less than failure-avoidance to the tech.
rows.sat_rat: Calendar-based PM inevitably replaces some good parts early, an accepted inefficiency in the status quo.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of lingering unease that directionality is not truly restored when the upset is declared resolved
rows.imp_rat: Lingering unease that an upset isn't truly resolved weighs on the tech but is secondary to the physical restoration itself.
rows.sat_rat: A stable post-restoration reading gives partial relief, though momentary-vs-sustained ambiguity leaves residual doubt.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time spent anxious about contamination before correct directionality can be confirmed restored
rows.imp_rat: Time spent anxious about contamination before confirming restoration is a genuine but softer stake than the physical restoration outcome.
rows.sat_rat: Quick confirmation once flow re-establishes limits the anxious window, though it is not eliminated today.

rows.stmt: Reduce the amount of maintenance labor consumed sustaining the pressure cascade beyond what reliability requires
rows.imp_rat: Trimming maintenance labor beyond reliability needs serves resource optimization but is peripheral to guaranteeing containment.
rows.sat_rat: Maintenance is largely calendar-based and over-provisioned, so labor efficiency around the cascade is only moderately addressed today.

rows.stmt: Minimize the percent of HVAC energy expended overcoming avoidable airflow losses across classified zones
rows.imp_rat: HVAC energy waste matters to this manager's resource-allocation duty but is clearly secondary to actually holding the cascade.
rows.sat_rat: Some sites monitor fan energy and rebalance, but avoidable airflow losses are rarely quantified or actively driven down today.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of unpredictability in the staffing and parts needed to keep the cascade within limits across shifts
rows.imp_rat: Predictable staffing and parts across shifts helps the manager plan, but it is a supporting concern rather than the containment core.
rows.sat_rat: Cross-shift demand for cascade upkeep is largely reactive and hard to forecast, leaving unpredictability only partly tamed.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree to which engineering peers attribute a successful cascade design to someone other than this role
rows.imp_rat: Getting professional credit for a successful cascade design matters to his standing but is a softer status concern than avoiding blame for failures.
rows.sat_rat: Recognition of design authorship is inconsistent in multidisciplinary projects where success is often attributed to the integrator or contractor.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time lost to vendor qualification and bid evaluation during procurement of the pressure cascade control capability
rows.imp_rat: Time lost to vendor qualification and bid evaluation affects project cycle time but is a process-efficiency concern rather than a core-value risk.
rows.sat_rat: Established supplier-qualification workflows handle this adequately though they remain labor-intensive in a regulated environment.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time the funding request waits in the capital approval queue against competing project demands
rows.imp_rat: Queue time in the capital approval process delays the acquisition but is largely outside the manager's direct control and not a value-destroying error.
rows.sat_rat: Stage-gate capital processes move at fixed cadences, giving predictable but not fast approval outcomes.

rows.source_job: feel confident the case will hold
rows.stmt: Reduce the likelihood of being blindsided by an unanticipated question that exposes a gap in the justification
rows.imp_rat: Being blindsided in review is an emotional/reputational concern but secondary to the substantive budget and delivery outcomes.
rows.sat_rat: Rehearsal and stakeholder pre-alignment help, yet unanticipated questions in capital committees still occur.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of procurement outcomes that undermine the perception of consistently securing best-value capability
rows.imp_rat: Perception of consistently securing best value supports professional standing but is a status concern subordinate to the functional job.
rows.sat_rat: Track records build gradually, and current best-value evidence for controls buys is adequate but not compelling.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time spent reconciling competing funding requests to release capital for the pressure-cascade system
rows.imp_rat: Reconciling competing funding requests consumes cycle time but is a procedural burden rather than a core stakes driver for the cascade decision.
rows.sat_rat: Established capital-committee processes provide a path to resolution, though contamination items still get caught in prolonged negotiation.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree to which one's contribution to unbroken directional integrity goes unrecognized across the classified zones
rows.imp_rat: Recognition for sustained integrity matters to identity but is peripheral to the tech's functional accountability for the cascade.
rows.sat_rat: Invisible, always-working infrastructure rarely earns recognition, so this goes largely unmet today.
